title: Thermistor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The dryer thermistor (temperature sensor) circuit is reading as open.
fullDescription: Error E1 indicates the dryer thermistor circuit has an open condition. The thermistor monitors drum air temperature to regulate heating. When the control board cannot read a temperature signal, it shuts down the heater for safety and halts the cycle.

causes.cause: Thermistor has failed open
Thermistor wiring harness is disconnected or broken
Thermistor connector is corroded from lint and moisture
Control board temperature input circuit has failed

symptoms: E1 error on display
Dryer will not heat
Dryer stops mid-cycle
Clothes come out damp
Auto-dry cycles do not function properly

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: The thermistor is typically mounted on the blower housing or inside the drum housing. Unplug the dryer and check the connector for looseness or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 10,000 ohms (10k). OL means the thermistor is open.

fixSteps.title: Check the wiring harness
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wires from the thermistor to the control board for breaks, especially where they pass through the drum housing or near heat sources.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the old thermistor, remove the mounting screw, and install the replacement. Reconnect the wiring connector.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the thermistor does not clear E1, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E1 thermistor open error fix

faq.q: What is a thermistor?
faq.a: A thermistor is a temperature-sensitive resistor. Its resistance changes with temperature, allowing the control board to monitor dryer air temperature.

faq.q: Can lint cause E1?
faq.a: Lint buildup can damage thermistor wiring or cause overheating that degrades the sensor over time. Regular lint cleaning is important.

faq.q: Is E1 the same on all GE dryers?
faq.a: E1 means thermistor open on most GE dryer models, but always check your specific model's technical sheet to confirm.

title: Thermistor Shorted
shortDescription: The dryer thermistor is reading a shorted condition with abnormally low resistance.
fullDescription: Error E2 indicates the dryer thermistor has a short circuit, reading near zero resistance. The control board interprets this as an extremely high temperature and shuts down heating. This is the opposite condition from E1 (open circuit).

causes.cause: Thermistor has internally shorted
Thermistor wiring is pinched, causing bare wires to touch
Moisture in the thermistor connector
Control board input circuit has failed

symptoms: E2 error on display
Dryer will not heat
Dryer stops shortly after starting
Display may show abnormally high temperature reading
Clothes remain wet

fixSteps.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Disconnect the thermistor and measure resistance. Should be approximately 10,000 ohms at room temperature. Near zero indicates a short.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the thermistor connector for condensation or water. Dry with compressed air. Moisture between leads can create a low-resistance path.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring for shorts
fixSteps.instruction: Check thermistor wires for damaged insulation where bare conductors may be touching each other or metal parts of the dryer.

fixSteps.title: Replace thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old thermistor and install the replacement. Ensure proper routing of wires away from heat sources.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the thermistor and checking wiring does not resolve E2.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E2 thermistor shorted error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E1 and E2?
faq.a: E1 means the thermistor circuit is open (infinite resistance). E2 means it is shorted (near zero resistance). Both require thermistor replacement.

faq.q: Can running the dryer with a blocked vent cause E2?
faq.a: A blocked vent causes overheating that can damage the thermistor, eventually leading to E2.

faq.q: Is the thermistor the same as the thermal fuse?
faq.a: No. The thermistor is a variable-resistance sensor. The thermal fuse is a one-time safety device that blows when temperature is too high.

title: Exhaust High Limit Thermostat Tripped
shortDescription: The exhaust high-limit thermostat has detected excessive exhaust temperature.
fullDescription: Error E3 indicates the exhaust high-limit thermostat has tripped due to excessive exhaust air temperature. This safety device protects against dryer fires by cutting off the heater when exhaust temperatures exceed safe limits, typically caused by restricted airflow.

causes.cause: Dryer vent is clogged with lint
Exhaust vent hose is kinked or crushed
Lint filter is clogged
High-limit thermostat has failed

symptoms: E3 error on display
Dryer shuts off after a few minutes of heating
Dryer runs but does not heat
Excessive heat around the dryer
Longer than normal drying times before E3 appears

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the lint filter thoroughly. If fabric softener sheets are used, wash the filter with soap and water to remove invisible residue that blocks airflow.

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the vent hose from the back of the dryer. Use a vent cleaning brush or vacuum to clear lint from the entire vent run to the outside. Check the exterior vent cap for blockage.

fixSteps.title: Check the vent hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the vent hose for kinks, crushing, or excessive length. Replace flexible foil hose with rigid or semi-rigid metal duct for better airflow.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the high-limit thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: If venting is clear but E3 persists, test the high-limit thermostat for continuity. Replace if open. Located on the blower housing or heater box.

whenToCallPro: If vent cleaning and thermostat replacement do not resolve E3, or if you cannot access the full vent run.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E3 high limit thermostat exhaust vent fix

faq.q: How often should I clean my dryer vent?
faq.a: At least once per year, or more often if you notice longer drying times. Homes with long vent runs should clean every 6 months.

faq.q: Can E3 cause a dryer fire?
faq.a: E3 is a safety code that prevents fire by shutting down the heater. However, the underlying cause (blocked vent) is a leading cause of dryer fires. Address immediately.

faq.q: Is fabric softener residue really a problem?
faq.a: Yes. Liquid fabric softener and dryer sheets leave an invisible film on the lint filter that significantly reduces airflow over time.

title: Inlet Thermistor Fault
shortDescription: The inlet (drum) thermistor is not providing valid readings.
fullDescription: Error E4 indicates a problem with the inlet thermistor, which measures the temperature of air entering the drum. The control board uses this reading along with the exhaust thermistor to optimize drying. A faulty inlet thermistor prevents the auto-dry moisture sensing system from working correctly.

causes.cause: Inlet thermistor has failed
Thermistor connector is loose or corroded
Wiring between inlet thermistor and board is damaged
Control board analog input has failed

symptoms: E4 error on display
Auto-dry cycles do not work properly
Dryer may overdry or underdry clothes
Dryer stops during auto-dry cycle
Timed dry may still work normally

fixSteps.title: Locate the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. The inlet thermistor is typically near the blower inlet or at the front of the drum housing. Consult your model's tech sheet for exact location.

fixSteps.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure resistance. Should read approximately 10,000 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wiring from the thermistor connector to the control board for damage, paying attention to areas near moving parts.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw or clip, disconnect the old thermistor, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor tests fine and E4 persists, the board needs diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E4 inlet thermistor error fix

faq.q: Can I use timed dry instead of auto-dry with E4?
faq.a: Yes. Timed dry does not rely on the moisture sensing system and should still work with E4 active.

faq.q: Are the inlet and exhaust thermistors the same part?
faq.a: On many GE dryers they use the same part number. However, they are located in different positions. Verify for your model.

faq.q: What does the inlet thermistor do?
faq.a: It measures the temperature of air entering the drum. Combined with exhaust temperature, the control determines how much heat is being absorbed by the clothes to estimate moisture level.

title: Motor Overload or Stall
shortDescription: The dryer motor has stalled or tripped its overload protection.
fullDescription: Error E5 indicates the dryer motor is drawing excessive current or has stalled. The motor overload protector may have tripped, or the control board detected that the motor is not spinning at the expected speed. Causes include mechanical obstructions, worn bearings, or a failing motor.

causes.cause: Drum is jammed by a foreign object caught between drum and housing
Worn drum support rollers or glides causing excessive friction
Drive belt is too tight or off-track
Motor bearings are worn and the motor is seizing

symptoms: E5 error on display
Dryer drum will not turn
Grinding or squealing noise before E5 appears
Motor hums but drum does not spin
Burning rubber smell from belt slipping

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and try to spin the drum by hand. If it does not rotate freely, something is jammed. Look between the drum and the front or rear housing for clothing or debris.

fixSteps.title: Inspect drum rollers and glides
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front or top panel to access the drum. Check the support rollers (rear) and drum glides (front) for wear. Replace if flat-spotted or worn.

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the belt for proper tension, cracks, or glazing. Ensure it is correctly routed around the drum, idler pulley, and motor pulley.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the drum spins freely by hand but the motor does not run, disconnect and test motor resistance. Also check the motor start capacitor if equipped.

whenToCallPro: If the motor itself has failed and needs replacement, or if you cannot identify the source of the mechanical resistance.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E5 motor stall drum not spinning fix

faq.q: Can a small item cause E5?
faq.a: Yes. A sock, coin, or other small item trapped between the drum and housing can jam the drum and trigger E5.

faq.q: How long do drum rollers last?
faq.a: Drum rollers typically last 10-15 years but can wear faster with heavy use. Replace all rollers at the same time.

faq.q: Can I lubricate the motor instead of replacing it?
faq.a: Some GE dryer motors have sealed bearings that cannot be lubricated. If the motor is seizing, replacement is necessary.

title: Communication Error Between Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and the user interface board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: Error E6 indicates a communication failure between the main control board (located at the rear or bottom) and the user interface board (behind the control panel). These boards exchange cycle data and user inputs over a serial communication link.

causes.cause: Wire harness between boards is loose or damaged
User interface board has failed
Main control board has failed
Power surge damaged communication circuits

symptoms: E6 error on display
Dryer will not respond to controls
Display may be blank or flashing
Dryer will not start
Random lights may illuminate on the control panel

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. This can reset the communication between the boards.

fixSteps.title: Check wire harness connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and control panel cover. Locate the ribbon cable or wire harness connecting the two boards. Reseat all connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the wire harness for pinched, cut, or melted wires. Check both board connectors for burned or corroded pins.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If the harness is intact, one board has failed. A technician can determine which. Replace the identified board and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connections does not resolve E6, a technician can test each board to identify the failure.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E6 communication error board fix

faq.q: Which board is more likely to fail?
faq.a: The main control board is slightly more likely to fail than the interface board, but both are common. Professional testing is the best way to identify the faulty board.

faq.q: Can I swap the boards to test?
faq.a: If you have access to a known-good board of the same part number, swapping can help identify the failed board. However, this is usually not practical.

faq.q: Is E6 common on GE dryers?
faq.a: E6 is moderately common, especially on models that have experienced power surges. A surge protector is recommended.

title: Heater Relay Failure
shortDescription: The heater relay on the control board has failed.
fullDescription: Error E8 indicates the heater relay on the main control board is not functioning correctly. The relay may be stuck open (dryer will not heat) or stuck closed (dryer heats continuously). This is a control board level failure requiring board replacement.

causes.cause: Heater relay contacts are welded or burned
Excessive current from a shorted heating element damaged the relay
Power surge damaged the relay
Board-level component failure near the relay

symptoms: E8 error on display
Dryer does not heat
Dryer heats continuously and will not stop
Burning smell from the control area
Dryer shuts down for safety

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. If E8 clears temporarily, monitor closely for recurrence.

fixSteps.title: Check the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Before replacing the board, test the heating element resistance. A shorted element (very low resistance) can damage a new board. Normal resistance is 8-20 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned or discolored components around the relay area on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove all connectors from the old board, noting positions. Swap in the new board and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the dryer heats continuously and will not stop, unplug immediately and call a professional. This is a fire hazard.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E8 heater relay board replacement fix

faq.q: Is a stuck closed relay dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. A relay stuck in the closed position means the heater runs continuously even when it should be off. This is a fire hazard. Unplug immediately.

faq.q: Should I replace the element too?
faq.a: Test the element first. If its resistance is normal, only the board needs replacement. If the element is shorted, replace both.

faq.q: Can the relay be replaced individually?
faq.a: Technically yes with soldering equipment, but replacing the full board is recommended for reliability.

title: Gas Valve or Igniter Failure (Gas Models)
shortDescription: The gas ignition system has failed to light within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error E9 on GE gas dryers indicates the ignition system did not successfully light the gas burner within the allowed time. The control board monitors the igniter and flame sensor to verify ignition. If the burner does not light after multiple attempts, E9 is displayed.

causes.cause: Gas igniter has weakened and cannot reach ignition temperature
Gas valve solenoid coils have failed
Flame sensor is dirty or defective
Gas supply is turned off or low pressure

symptoms: E9 error on display
Dryer runs but does not heat (gas model)
Igniter glows but gas does not light
Intermittent heating that gets worse over time
Burning smell if ignition is delayed

fixSteps.title: Verify gas supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the gas shutoff valve behind or next to the dryer is fully open. Check that other gas appliances in the home are working.

fixSteps.title: Test the igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Observe the igniter during a heat cycle. It should glow bright orange-white. If it glows dimly or not at all, it needs replacement. It should draw 3-4 amps.

fixSteps.title: Test gas valve coils
fixSteps.instruction: If the igniter glows brightly but gas does not flow, the gas valve solenoid coils are likely faulty. Test each coil for resistance (approximately 1000-2000 ohms each).

fixSteps.title: Replace igniter and/or gas valve coils
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the igniter if weak, or the gas valve coils if they test bad. Both are common failure points. Many technicians replace both together.

whenToCallPro: If you smell gas but the burner will not light, turn off the gas supply and call a professional or your gas company immediately.
videoSearchQuery: GE gas dryer E9 igniter gas valve coil fix

faq.q: Why does my dryer heat sometimes but not always?
faq.a: This is a classic symptom of failing gas valve coils. They work when cool but fail when hot. The problem gradually worsens until heat stops completely.

faq.q: Is it safe to work on a gas dryer?
faq.a: Gas dryer repairs require caution. Always turn off gas before working on the ignition system. If you smell gas at any point, stop and call your gas company.

faq.q: Should I replace both the igniter and coils?
faq.a: If the dryer is more than 5 years old, many technicians recommend replacing both together since they wear at similar rates.

title: Drum Light Circuit Error
shortDescription: The drum light circuit has a fault.
fullDescription: Error E10 indicates the drum light circuit on the main control board has detected an abnormal condition. This could be a shorted light bulb, a wiring short in the drum light circuit, or a board-level component failure. While not critical to drying function, the error may prevent the dryer from operating.

causes.cause: Drum light bulb is shorted
Wiring to the drum light is shorted or damaged
Drum light socket is corroded
Control board drum light driver has failed

symptoms: E10 error on display
Drum light does not work
Dryer may not start due to the error
Error appears when door is opened
Occasional flickering of the drum light before failure

fixSteps.title: Replace the drum light bulb
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and locate the drum light. Replace the bulb with the correct type for your model. Use an appliance-rated bulb.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the light socket
fixSteps.instruction: Check the drum light socket for corrosion, loose contacts, or damage. Clean or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Check light wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring from the light socket to the control board. Look for damaged insulation or pinched wires.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: After addressing the light issue, unplug the dryer for 5 minutes and restart. If E10 persists, the control board light driver may have failed.

whenToCallPro: If the light bulb and wiring are fine but E10 persists, the board needs diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E10 drum light error fix

faq.q: Can I use a regular light bulb?
faq.a: No. Use only an appliance-rated bulb designed for the vibration and temperature inside a dryer. Regular bulbs may shatter.

faq.q: Does E10 prevent drying?
faq.a: On some models, E10 will prevent the dryer from starting. On others, you can continue to dry despite the error.

faq.q: Are GE dryer drum lights LED?
faq.a: Newer GE dryers may use LED drum lights. Older models use incandescent appliance bulbs. Check your model specifications.

title: Power Supply Voltage Error
shortDescription: The dryer is not receiving the correct supply voltage.
fullDescription: Error E11 indicates the main control board has detected an abnormal supply voltage. Electric dryers require 240V, and gas dryers require 120V. If the voltage is too high or too low, or if one leg of a 240V supply is missing, E11 is displayed to protect the dryer electronics and heating system.

causes.cause: One leg of the 240V supply has been lost (electric dryer)
Circuit breaker is partially tripped
Loose connection at the dryer terminal block
Home electrical supply voltage is out of range

symptoms: E11 error on display
Dryer will not start or heat
Display may appear dim
Motor runs slowly or not at all
Error may come and go during brownouts

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the dryer circuit breaker in your electrical panel. It should be a double-pole (240V) breaker for electric dryers. Turn it fully off and then back on. A partially tripped breaker can supply only 120V.

fixSteps.title: Test outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, test the dryer outlet. Between the two hot slots you should read 240V. Between each hot and neutral or ground should read 120V.

fixSteps.title: Check the terminal block
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and remove the rear access panel to the terminal block. Ensure the power cord connections are tight and not corroded.

fixSteps.title: Contact an electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If voltage at the outlet is incorrect, the problem is in your home electrical system. Contact a licensed electrician to diagnose and repair.

whenToCallPro: If the outlet voltage is not correct, call an electrician. Do not attempt to repair home wiring yourself.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E11 voltage error 240V power fix

faq.q: What causes one leg of 240V to fail?
faq.a: A partially tripped breaker, a loose connection in the electrical panel, or a utility transformer issue can cause one of the two 120V legs to drop.

faq.q: Can E11 damage my dryer?
faq.a: Running on incorrect voltage can damage the motor and electronics. The E11 error is designed to prevent this damage by shutting down the dryer.

faq.q: Can I use an extension cord with my dryer?
faq.a: Never use an extension cord with a dryer. The high current draw can cause voltage drop, overheating, and fire risk.

title: Door Switch Circuit Error
shortDescription: The door switch is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error E12 indicates the dryer door switch circuit has a fault. The door switch tells the control board whether the door is open or closed. If the switch signal is stuck, intermittent, or contradictory, the dryer will not operate for safety reasons.

causes.cause: Door switch has failed
Door switch actuator is broken or misaligned
Wiring to the door switch is damaged
Control board door switch input has failed

symptoms: E12 error on display
Dryer will not start
Dryer starts but immediately stops
Drum light does not turn on when door is opened
Door does not seem to latch properly

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and press the door switch button by hand. You should hear a click. If no click, the switch may be stuck.

fixSteps.title: Check switch continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and access the door switch. Test continuity: should be closed when pressed (door shut) and open when released (door open).

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door strike
fixSteps.instruction: Check the door strike (the part of the door that presses the switch). If it is broken or worn, the switch may not fully engage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front panel or top panel to access the switch. Disconnect wires, remove the old switch, and install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the switch and wiring are fine but E12 persists, the control board input needs professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: GE dryer E12 door switch error fix replacement

faq.q: Can I bypass the door switch?
faq.a: Never bypass the door switch. It is a safety device that prevents the drum from spinning with the door open, which could cause injury.

faq.q: Why does my dryer stop right after starting?
faq.a: A failing door switch can momentarily register as open right after the cycle begins, causing the dryer to stop immediately.

faq.q: Is the door switch the same as the door latch?
faq.a: They are different. The switch is an electrical component that detects door position. The latch is the mechanical catch that holds the door closed.
